title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chatbots And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's strategic direction including merging chatbots with agents, the role of compute as a limiting resource, and the vision of persistent agents as the interface of the future. He predicts pricing pressure as premium intelligence becomes commodity, competitive dynamics with Microsoft, and highlights health as a priority domain for AI impact.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz interviews OpenAI president and co-founder Greg Brockman about the company's strategic direction, centered on merging chatbots with agents and treating persistent agents as the interface of the future. Brockman frames compute as a limiting resource and predicts pricing pressure as premium intelligence turns into a commodity. He also addresses competitive dynamics with Microsoft and names health as a priority domain for AI impact.
